sprint: Traveller Interaction Polish — Tap Hint, Cinzel, and a Base for the Greeting
task: Three concrete follow-ups on the just-shipped single-video Traveller pipeline. (1) "first move tap to continue towards right bottom corner" -- .gateway-tap-hint moved from bottom-center (bottom:36px, left:50%, transform:translateX(-50%)) to bottom-right (bottom:26px, right:28px). (2) "the font and color of text are not matching the scene" -- the greeting bubble's plain body sans-serif (Arial, inherited) and flat cream tone read as generic UI copy against the ornate carved-stone/gold-inscription banners in the real Gate footage ("The Adventure Awaits" / "Dream Believe Discover"). (3) "something royal and mystical at same time. use old story fonts" + "give this some base" -- Cinzel (a real Roman-inscription-style display serif, OFL-licensed, fetched fresh from Google Fonts and self-hosted at assets/fonts/cinzel-600-latin.woff2 alongside the existing Caveat/Kalam/Nunito set, since none of those read as royal/mystical) replaces the generic Georgia fallback stack entirely; --gw-gold (the same tone the Gate's own particles/sparks/threshold-flash already use) stays, now paired with a genuine gold glow (text-shadow) instead of a flat black drop shadow. A slim gold gradient rule (.gateway-greeting-bubble::after) sits beneath each line -- literally 'a base' for the words to stand on, like the foundation of an inscribed plaque -- its own width scaling proportionally to each line's own rendered width (a % of the bubble's own content-hugging box, capped at 210px so a long line doesn't run too wide), verified directly: 210px (capped) for 'Welcome, Traveller.' vs 145px for the shorter 'I am Lumo.', both genuinely ~64% of their own bubble's measured width. No separate animation needed on the pseudo-element -- the parent's existing opacity/transform keyframe already composites the whole box (text + pseudo-element) together, so the rule fades in/out in lockstep with the words for free. Verified via Playwright: the tap hint's bounding rect sits within 60px of both the right and bottom viewport edges; document.fonts.check('600 20px Cinzel') resolves true (the font genuinely loaded, not silently falling back); the underline width scales correctly per line; a full re-run of the existing 15-assertion Traveller/Returning-Creator pipeline suite plus the completion/tap-to-skip sanity checks and both goldenBuild.js suites all pass unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to css/style.css, assets/fonts/fonts.css, one new self-hosted font file, with a matching cache-bust version bump on both stylesheet <link> tags in index.html. The user's own separately-flagged pacing note ("its good. a bit racing. but we can fix that") is explicitly deferred, not addressed this pass.
